flashcards.front: What is the most common data type generated by IoT sensors?
flashcards.back: Time-series data. It is a sequence of data points indexed in time order (e.g., temperature readings taken every minute).

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between structured and semi-structured data?
flashcards.back: Structured data has a fixed schema (e.g., a database table). Semi-structured data has flexible or self-describing structure (e.g., JSON, XML).

flashcards.front: Why is JSON so prevalent in IoT communication?
flashcards.back: It is a lightweight, human-readable, semi-structured format that is easy for machines to parse and generate. It is the standard payload for protocols like MQTT and HTTP REST APIs.

flashcards.front: When would you choose Protocol Buffers over JSON?
flashcards.back: In scenarios requiring high efficiency: higher data volume frequencies, limited network bandwidth (e.g., LPWAN), or need for faster serialization/deserialization.

flashcards.front: What is the role of a Queue data structure in an IoT edge device?
flashcards.back: To decouple producer (sensor reading) and consumer (data transmission) tasks, acting as a FIFO buffer to prevent data loss and manage flow in real-time systems.
